bullets.text: Iran’s leadership is reportedly fractured and struggling to coordinate during conflict with the U.S., complicating decision-making and raising escalation/miscalculation risks.
A new U.S. missile strike hit an Iranian sports hall area, underscoring expanded target sets and increasing scrutiny over civilian harm and proportionality.
Threats of Strait of Hormuz disruption and tanker attacks lifted oil prices, with Chinese suppliers warning higher U.S. consumer prices via shipping and energy costs.
Markets whipsawed: futures rose and oil fell on reports Trump may end the war, yet analysts warn U.S. equities have further downside than past shocks.
Fed Chair Powell signaled no rate hikes just for an oil shock, saying inflation expectations remain anchored while monitoring private-credit stress.
European economic and consumer confidence weakened sharply amid Iran-war uncertainty, increasing recession risks for energy-importing economies.
Major Middle East oil producers have been selling U.S. Treasurys, potentially reflecting reserve diversification and geopolitics—adding marginal upward pressure to U.S. yields.
China’s March factory activity returned to growth at the fastest pace in a year, supporting global demand—but remains vulnerable to energy and trade shocks.
Sysco’s $29B buyout bet on ‘cash-and-carry’ signals food-service consolidation as consumer spending softens after most tax refunds are already spent.
States are pushing ahead with A.I. rules; California’s executive order may set a de facto baseline, but creates a fragmented U.S. compliance landscape.
A woman jailed five months after an A.I. match in a fraud case highlights high-stakes error rates in facial recognition and the need for oversight.
A.I. compute race accelerates: Mistral raised $830M debt for a Paris-area data center; Samsung-backed Rebellions raised $400M pre-IPO for chips.
JWST observed a mysterious explosion that defies current physics models, potentially forcing revisions in understanding of extreme cosmic transients.
A powerful cholesterol drug cut heart attack risk by 31% in new research, signaling potential shifts in cardiovascular care and large-scale drug demand.
